L1.area: Product management and entity form handling

L2.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where user-supplied HTTP request parameters are passed directly into database queries without proper type-safe sanitization, allowing remote attackers to execute arbitrary SQL commands.

L3.area: Product entity editing pages and the associated third-party form selection helpers
L3.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where user-supplied HTTP request parameters for product status and metadata fields are passed directly into database queries without type-safe filtering, allowing remote attackers to execute arbitrary SQL commands via crafted parameter values submitted through the product editing form.

ground_truth.reason: User-supplied HTTP parameters such as product status fields are retrieved without type-safe filtering and interpolated directly into SQL queries, allowing remote attackers to inject arbitrary SQL commands.
